entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Practices aimed at managing natural resources sustainably to impact water quantity and quality

entities.name: WATER QUANTITY
entities.description: The amount of water available in a given area, including surface and groundwater

entities.name: WATER QUALITY
entities.description: The chemical, physical, and biological characteristics of water

entities.name: BIOPHYSICAL INDICATORS
entities.description: Measurable indicators used to assess the impacts of interventions on water quantity and quality

entities.name: SURFACE WATER
entities.description: Water on the surface of the planet, such as in rivers and lakes

entities.name: GROUNDWATER
entities.description: Water located beneath the earth's surface in soil pore spaces and fractures of rock formations

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Specific actions or technologies applied in agriculture to improve outcomes

entities.name: NRM INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Specific actions applied in natural resource management to improve outcomes

entities.name: SEMIARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures where case studies were drawn from

entities.name: MONITORING
entities.description: The process of observing and checking the biophysical impacts of technological and resource management interventions
